description:
You are an edge-computing engineer about to push two binary
    update-packages to several hundred IoT gateways.  
    A QA requirement says every deployment batch must contain:

        1. A self-contained copy of the packages that will actually
           be sent to the devices
        2. A machine-readable manifest that records the canonical
           SHA-256 checksum and size (in raw bytes) of every
           package
        3. A plain-text log proving that you re-computed those
           checksums locally and that they matched the manifest

    The packages have already been staged for you under

        /home/user/preloaded_packages/

        ├── sensor_utils-1.2.3.tar.gz
        └── edge_comm-0.9.8.tar.gz

    For reproducible tests the two files are intentionally empty
    (0-byte length).  Do **NOT** modify their contents.

    Your job is to prepare the final delivery folder
    /home/user/iot_deploy/ with the following structure and
    artefacts:

        /home/user/iot_deploy/
        ├── packages/                       (exact name, lowercase)
        │   ├── sensor_utils-1.2.3.tar.gz   (identical to source)
        │   └── edge_comm-0.9.8.tar.gz      (identical to source)
        ├── manifest.json
        └── checksum_verification.log

    Detailed requirements
    ---------------------

    1. packages directory
       • Must contain a byte-for-byte copy of the two *.tar.gz
         files located in /home/user/preloaded_packages/.
       • Do not place any other files in this directory.

    2. manifest.json
       • Must be valid JSON (no comments).
       • Use 4-space indentation and a trailing newline.
       • Exact schema (order of keys matters!):

            {
                "packages": [
                    {
                        "name": "sensor_utils",
                        "version": "1.2.3",
                        "checksum_sha256": "<64 hexadecimal chars>",
                        "size_bytes": 0
                    },
                    {
                        "name": "edge_comm",
                        "version": "0.9.8",
                        "checksum_sha256": "<64 hexadecimal chars>",
                        "size_bytes": 0
                    }
                ]
            }

       • The checksum values **must** be the canonical SHA-256
         digest of the corresponding file that lives inside
         /home/user/iot_deploy/packages/.
       • size_bytes must be derived from `stat --format=%s`.

    3. checksum_verification.log
       • A plain-text file using Unix line-endings (`\n`).
       • Each line is the output of a successful checksum
         comparison and **must** follow this exact template:

             <filename> OK

         where <filename> is the basename only
         (e.g. sensor_utils-1.2.3.tar.gz).
       • The file order must be alphabetical:

             edge_comm-0.9.8.tar.gz OK
             sensor_utils-1.2.3.tar.gz OK

       • End the file with a single newline.

    Concurrency requirement
    -----------------------
    Management asked you to demonstrate that checksum generation
    can be parallelised.  
    Therefore:
      • Spawn the two `sha256sum` calculations as background
        jobs that run in parallel.
      • Make sure your script waits for both jobs to finish before
        you write manifest.json or checksum_verification.log, so
        the digests recorded in those files are final and correct.

    Deliverable
    -----------
    When you have finished, the automated grader will:

      1. Check that /home/user/iot_deploy/packages/ exists and
         contains exactly the two files specified above.
      2. Parse manifest.json and verify:
         • The JSON syntax and indentation.
         • The presence and **order** of all required keys.
         • That size_bytes equals the actual file size.
         • That checksum_sha256 matches the real digest.
      3. Read checksum_verification.log and verify:
         • Exact alphabetical order.
         • Exact text `<filename> OK`
         • One trailing newline, no extra blank lines.
      4. Ensure you used background jobs by checking
         your shell history for an ampersand (`&`) immediately
         following each `sha256sum` command.

    No super-user (root) privileges are available, and no network
    access is allowed inside the container.  All paths must be
    explicit; avoid using `~`.

    Good luck, and happy deploying!
truth:
<!-- Pre-loaded artefacts that already exist when the task begins -->
    <preloaded_files>
        <file path="/home/user/preloaded_packages/sensor_utils-1.2.3.tar.gz" size_bytes="0" sha256="e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855"/>
        <file path="/home/user/preloaded_packages/edge_comm-0.9.8.tar.gz" size_bytes="0" sha256="e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855"/>
    </preloaded_files>

    <!-- Expected final files created by the agent -->
    <expected_output>
        <directory path="/home/user/iot_deploy/packages"/>
        <file path="/home/user/iot_deploy/packages/sensor_utils-1.2.3.tar.gz" size_bytes="0" sha256="e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855"/>
        <file path="/home/user/iot_deploy/packages/edge_comm-0.9.8.tar.gz" size_bytes="0" sha256="e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855"/>

        <file path="/home/user/iot_deploy/manifest.json">
            <![CDATA[
{
    "packages": [
        {
            "name": "sensor_utils",
            "version": "1.2.3",
            "checksum_sha256": "e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855",
            "size_bytes": 0
        },
        {
            "name": "edge_comm",
            "version": "0.9.8",
            "checksum_sha256": "e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855",
            "size_bytes": 0
        }
    ]
}
]]>
        </file>

        <file path="/home/user/iot_deploy/checksum_verification.log">
            <![CDATA[
edge_comm-0.9.8.tar.gz OK
sensor_utils-1.2.3.tar.gz OK
]]>
        </file>
    </expected_output>

    <!-- Auxiliary grading data -->
    <grading>
        <!-- The grader will recompute each checksum to be safe,
             but we still list the canonical digests here. -->
        <sha256_empty_file>e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855</sha256_empty_file>
    </grading>
